summary: Backend pytest coverage added for the new daily auto-generator on AI City Landing Pages. 14/14 tests pass (auth gating, GET envelope, PUT partial-update + 6 out-of-range 422 cases, run-now happy path with idempotent last_run_date, full drain_email_sent lifecycle via direct Mongo sandbox, and scheduler job registration). All changes to DB / settings doc are reverted on teardown.
critical_code_review_comments: services/city_pages_autogen.py: when queue is empty AND drain_email_sent is already True, the function returns {ran:true, queue_empty:true} but does NOT update last_run_date. This means the hourly probe will re-enter the empty branch every hour (cheap, but the last_run_date gate stops being meaningful for empty days). Consider also setting last_run_date in the 'queue empty + already sent' branch for consistency.

services/city_pages_autogen.py:166: daily_count is silently clamped to 1..25 inside the tick even though the AutogenSettings pydantic model already enforces 1..25 via Field(ge=1, le=25). Defensive but redundant; either drop the clamp or document it as a fallback for legacy rows.

routes/city_landing_pages.py:471 AutogenSettings: all three fields are Optional with default=None to support partial PATCH-style PUTs. This works but is non-standard REST (PUT usually = full replace). Consider renaming to PATCH or adding a docstring note in the OpenAPI description.

scheduler.py:1040 CronTrigger(minute=25) — hourly probe is correct, but if hour_utc is changed to current UTC hour AFTER minute 25, the new value won't fire until next hour. Acceptable (matches ticket design) but worth noting in the admin UI tooltip.
seed_data_creation: None permanent. Drain test temporarily flips all pending → generated with `_test_drain_marker=true`, then reverts on finally{}. Settings doc snapshotted + replaced on teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_city_pages_autogen.py is self-contained and idempotent — safe to re-run. The drain test does NOT spend an LLM call when running on an empty queue (the early return covers both paths). The single run-now test clamps daily_count to 1 to keep the LLM cost minimal (~10s). If you want to add edge cases later: (a) verify drain_email_sent flag flip happens BEFORE generation when force=True on a pending queue; (b) verify settings.last_run_message string format. Both endpoints are wired through Depends(get_current_user) + _is_admin() so 401 responses come back as 403 once a valid non-admin token is supplied — auth gating tests assert in (401, 403) to be tolerant.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. All seven feature-test items in the review request pass.
